seoTitle: Mount Pleasant vs Mount Rankin — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mount Pleasant and Mount Rankin. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mount Pleasant vs Mount Rankin: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mount Pleasant and Mount Rankin.
heading: Mount Pleasant vs Mount Rankin — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mount Pleasant, New South Wales and Mount Rankin, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mount Rankin has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Mount Pleasant (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mount Pleasant or Mount Rankin safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mount Pleasant has a flood risk score of 27/100, while Mount Rankin scores 46/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mount Pleasant scores 33/100 for bushfire risk. Mount Rankin scores 42/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mount Pleasant or Mount Rankin?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mount Rankin has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.13% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
